items.title: Six years on, officers relive one of Victoria Police's darkest days

children.content: The deaths of four Victoria Police officers on April 22, 2020 sent shock waves through the force, with the impacts of Eastern Freeway tragedy still felt six years on.

items.title: Man dies in fuel tanker rollover on freeway east of Melbourne

children.content: Police say the Princes Freeway will remain closed into the night, as emergency services drain fuel from the tanker and clean up the crash site.

image.altText: Emergency services attending a fuel tanker crash in the rain.

items.title: Sisters separated in Afghanistan are becoming Australian citizens

children.content: Five years ago, Adiba was separated from her sister Arya as she fled Afghanistan. Now the Ganji siblings have reunited in Melbourne and are on their way to obtaining Australian citizenship.

image.altText: Adiba and Arya, two sisters, holding hands, smiling

items.title: Melbourne woman suffers acute liver failure after using knock-off drug

children.content: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

gallery.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

thumbnail.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

items.title: Transatlantic slave trade gravest crime against humanity, Juneteenth marks its end

children.content: Americans are commemorating Juneteenth months after a UN resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.

image.altText: The side profile of a woman's face. She's wearing Black Lives Matter earrings with a woman at centre, fist raised

items.title: Why these retirees won't give up the 3:30am starts and 14-hour shifts

children.content: When the alarm goes off at 3:30am in this freezing outback pit-stop, it's music to the ears of the volunteer bakers who happily spend their winters in a bakery like no other.

image.altText: A man in a cap laughing in a kitchen with women laughing behind him.

items.title: How police are unravelling Dezi Freeman's support network

children.content: As police continue the investigation into Dezi Freeman's support network, experts say those who aided him might have shared similar ideology.

gallery.altText: Two police walk holding seized items.

thumbnail.altText: Two police walk holding seized items.

items.title: Socceroos live events marred by flares, alleged sexual assault

children.content: Thousands of people flocked to sites across Australia to show their support as the Socceroos fall short against World Cup host USA.

items.title: Hawks break drought at Carrara, winning tough encounter against Suns

children.content: The Hawks win at Carrara for the first time since 2014, after a tough, physical encounter against the Suns.

image.altText: A grinning Hawthorn AFL player clenches his fist in celebration after a goal.

items.title: Farmstay guest found guilty of killing host who rejected his advances

children.content: A jury has found Timothy Loosemore guilty of manslaughter over the death of Maree Vermont in Goldie, central Victoria.

image.altText: A man being led into a court building from a van in handcuffs

items.title: Superbikes move leaves Phillip Island community in limbo

children.content: The Phillip Island community is coming to terms with the loss of another major sporting event with South Australia claiming the rights to Australia's Superbike World Championship race from 2028.

gallery.altText: A line of three World Superbike riders driver round a corner through the rain.

thumbnail.altText: A line of three World Superbike riders driver round a corner through the rain.

items.title: AFL matriarch helped Freeman's wife get to Australia, stored family's guns

children.content: Kay Reid, the mother of premiership players Ben and Sam Reid, reveals that she and her husband Bruce's deep ties to police killer Dezi Freeman and his family date back two decades.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: So-called ISIS bride believes terrorist group 'sucks', lawyer says

children.content: Police fear a so-called ISIS bride who recently returned to Australia will spread extremist ideology if she is freed on bail, but her lawyer says that will not be the case.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: More soft plastics needed as Australia's recycling capacity booms

children.content: Australia's soft plastics recycling industry is operating at nearly triple the capacity it was at now-collapsed national recycler REDcycle's peak, but consumers are yet to be convinced.

gallery.altText: A close up of shredded recycled soft plastic.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of shredded recycled soft plastic.

items.title: Third person charged over Melbourne synagogue fire

children.content: Police have charged a 20-year-old from Melbourne's north-west with a number of charges including arson over a fire that destroyed the Adass Israel Synagogue in 2024.

image.altText: Firefights walk past fire damage and smashed glass at the Synagogue.

items.title: Union won't rule out more strikes as Victorian teachers reject pay offer

children.content: State school teachers in Victoria vote to reject the government's 28 to 32 per cent pay rise, despite their union's stance.

image.altText: Man holds a placard reading 'lowest funded schools, lowest paid teachers' at a rally in Melbourne.

items.title: Lal Lal Falls runs dry as upstream 'greed' sees water vanish

children.content: Indigenous and environmental groups are accusing a Chinese company of hoarding water on a Victorian property and causing a sacred waterfall to run dry.

image.altText: A man stands at the edge of a cliff

items.title: Coalition's renewable energy buffer zones risks projects, report says

children.content: Energy experts have mixed views on what the Victorian Coalition's proposed renewable energy buffer zone policy could mean for the state's energy transition.

image.altText: A landscape photo of a windfarm with white windmills and a cloudy sky.

items.title: Woman went on shopping spree after killing beloved neighbour, court hears

children.content: Milena Bogojevska spent more than $4,000 after stealing from the home of 85-year-old Lolene Whitehand who she fatally bashed then dumped in the Maribyrnong River.

gallery.altText: A closeup of a woman's face in a dark room.

thumbnail.altText: A closeup of a woman's face in a dark room.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Fire authorities failed to meet targets ahead of 2024 Grampians blazes

children.content: An auditor-general's report finds fire authorities missed planned fuel reduction targets before two disastrous fire seasons and warns properties could be at risk if strategic fuel breaks are not put in place.

image.altText: A crew of firefighters in full protective gear and high-visibility shirts look out onto the tarmac of an airport.

items.title: New data shines light on state of EV public charger network

children.content: It's often claimed Australia has an EV public charger shortage, but analysis of exclusive data shows most fast charger sites are not congested.

image.altText: EV charging site with empty bays.

items.title: Police investigating after geese mowed down by car in Victoria

children.content: Police are investigating whether a driver deliberately mowed down a gaggle of geese crossing a road in central Victoria.

gallery.altText: A gaggle of geese crossing a road.

thumbnail.altText: A gaggle of geese crossing a road.

avatar.alt: A headshot of Nate standing in front of a dimly lit building wearing a jacket.

items.title: How Socceroo Patrick Beach became the 'last line of defence' at World Cup

children.content: At just 22, Patrick Beach stunned the world in his FIFA World Cup debut and third international start with the Socceroos against Türkiye last weekend.

image.altText: A composite of Beach in Socceroos uniform pumping his fist in celebration next to him as a child playing soccer.

items.title: Push to turn NSW south coast into state's largest national park

children.content: The National Parks Association wants an ambitious 1.2-million-hectare national park spanning from the Illawarra to the Victorian border.

image.altText: A tiger quoll straddles a fallen tree branch

items.title: Appeal denied for woman who set friend on fire at drunken party

children.content: Corbie Jean Walpole, who set her friend on fire after he made a "misogynistic" comment at a party, has lost her bid for a shorter jail term, with the appeal judges saying her offending required "strong denunciation".

image.altText: Two women with sunglasses in the foreground and a man in the background

items.title: Eviction notice for elderly Melbourne renter ruled invalid

children.content: HousingFirst has lost its bid to repossess the inner-east Melbourne property of an elderly tenant who disputed the validity of a rental increase.

image.altText: The outside of Glenloch Terrace.

items.title: Virginia Trioli to leave the ABC after 27 years

children.content: After 27 years as a journalist, broadcaster and program maker at the ABC, Virginia Trioli announces she is leaving to pursue new creative opportunities.

gallery.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

thumbnail.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

items.title: Government buys back $430m worth of water for Murray-Darling Basin

children.content: A $430 million Murray-Darling Basin water buyback sparks a fresh political fight over the balance between food production and environmental recovery.

image.altText: An irrigation pump in the river, low water

items.title: Dockers down Cats as Bolton takes mark of the year contender

children.content: Fremantle comes from behind to beat Geelong for a 13th straight game, as Shai Bolton hits the heights at Perth Stadium with a Mark of the Year contender.

image.altText: A Fremantle AFL forward flies high and grabs the ball in mid-air, standing on a teammate and a Geelong player.

items.title: Magpies admit Hill is 'a long way away from playing footy'

children.content: Bobby Hill returns to Collingwood training, but coach Craig McRae says it will be some time before the 2023 Norm Smith medallist is ready to play in the AFL again.

image.altText: Bobby Hill at a Collingwood AFL training session in 2026.

items.title: Three pet dogs mourned after suspected baiting at popular walking spot

children.content: The dogs died within hours of eating an unknown substance while being walked at a trotting track in a regional Victorian town.

image.altText: Three dogs gathered outside a doorway.

items.title: AFL stars' mother recounts visit from Dezi Freeman detectives

children.content: Kay Reid, a regional Victorian businesswoman and the mother of footballers Ben and Sam Reid, confirms police searched her home as part of Taskforce Summit's investigation into the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman.

gallery.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

items.title: Victoria spruiks law and order policies despite rise in adult crime

children.content: Figures for the year to the end of March show youth crime fell by 6 per cent in Victoria, but adult crime rose by around 10 per cent.

image.altText: Victorian police officer from behind

items.title: Man charged with child grooming after detectives pose online as child

children.content: A 22-year-old man living on the NSW–Victorian border was arrested on Wednesday after detectives assumed the online identity of a girl.

image.altText: Silhouettes of figures at sunrise

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.
